claims.claim: Gemini 3.5 Flash includes native computer use as a built-in tool, not a separate model endpoint
Standard Gemini 3.5 Flash pricing: $1.50/1M input tokens, $9.00/1M output tokens (including thinking tokens)
Computer use on Gemini 3.5 Flash is charged as regular tokens at standard model pricing with no additional premium
Google DeepMind published the AI Control Roadmap on June 18, 2026, treating AI agents as insider threats
Gemini 3.5 Flash uses normalized 1000x1000 coordinate space for all computer use actions
Computer use supports three environment types: browser, mobile (phones), and desktop
Gemini 3.5 Flash has a 1M token context window

Seven built-in safety policy categories: FINANCIAL_TRANSACTIONS, COMMUNICATION_TOOL, ACCOUNT_CREATION, LEGAL_TERMS_AND_AGREEMENTS, USER_CONSENT_MANAGEMENT, SENSITIVE_DATA_MODIFICATION, DATA_MODIFICATION
Legacy Gemini 2.5 Computer Use Preview model at $1.25/$10.00 per 1M tokens (prompts ≤200k)
Gemini 3.5 Flash scored 78.4% on OSWorld-Verified benchmark for computer use tasks
Anthropic's computer use is implemented as tool calls within the Messages API
Anthropic warns computer use latency may be too slow for human-in-the-loop interaction
Google provides opt-in screenshot pixel scanner for adversarial prompt injection detection
Safety classifier returns three states per computer use action: regular/allowed, require_confirmation, or blocked
Batch API provides 50% cost reduction on Gemini 3.5 Flash: $0.75/1M input, $4.50/1M output
Free tier: 5,000 prompts per month free (shared across Gemini 3 models)
Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4.6: $3/1M input, $15/1M output — approximately 2x Gemini 3.5 Flash input cost
Google computer use uses the Interactions API (WebSocket), not the standard generateContent API
Claude Opus 4.8: $5/1M input, $25/1M output — over 3x Gemini 3.5 Flash input cost
Previously required standalone 2.5 Computer Use Preview model; now natively integrated in Gemini 3.5 Flash
